reviews.notes: [agent:unsupported_claim] Stephen's count is sourced, but the claims about apostolic reading and Church reception are not.
[agent:needs_source] The image works, but it adds factual and typological claims without support.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The covenant-language interpretation is not supported by the cited source or URL.
[agent:needs_source] POR is catchy, but the Goshen explanation is a factual claim without a source.
[agent:needs_rewrite] The POR hook is good, but the grain-and-granary detail is unsupported.
[agent:needs_source] The opening image is strong, but the promise-and-oath explanation is unsourced.
[agent:needs_source] The names hook is memorable, but the unnamed-Pharaoh claim should be sourced or cut.
[agent:needs_source] The final life-outruns-death line is a theological claim without support.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The measure-for-measure reward reading is not directly supported by the cited source.
[agent:needs_source] Nile-worship and sea-judgment claims need sources; the core river image is enough.
[agent:needs_rewrite] The trimester hook is workable, but the second-birth typology is extra and unsourced.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The 'same Greek word as Noah's ark' claim is not verified as written.
[agent:needs_source] The rescue scene is vivid, but the providence-heavy explanation should be sourced or trimmed.
[agent:needs_source] The paycheck hook is strong, but the extra redemption language is unsourced.
[agent:too_long] The key point is good, but the aid is too long and adds unsourced typology.
[agent:needs_source] The moral lesson is fine, but the core scene should stay tighter and sourced.
[agent:needs_source] The MIDian pun works, but the forty-year forge line needs a source.
[agent:needs_source] The bride-at-the-well pattern is interpretive and should not ride without support.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The two-name fact is fine, but calling Jethro a title is not verified here.
[agent:needs_source] The Hebrew-word note needs support; also, acceptableAnswers includes 'gershon,' which may be an internal inconsistency.
[agent:needs_source] The meaning is right, but the extra pilgrim language goes beyond the source.
[agent:needs_source] The explanation of divine remembering needs a source even though the verse itself is correct.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Adds the Sinai/same-height claim without auditable support.
[agent:needs_source] Strong Orthodox typology, but it has no usable URL and is too long.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Adds the 'dead skins' teaching without a source.
[agent:needs_source] The Gregory reading is useful, but it needs an auditable URL.
[agent:needs_source] Good Bible-pattern hook, but the cross-references need URLs.
[agent:needs_source] Excellent resurrection hook, but the Jesus citation needs a URL.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Ends with unsourced heavenly-homeland typology and runs long.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Adds an unsourced history lesson about Philistines and sea-peoples.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Adds the 'number of fullness' interpretation without a source.
[agent:needs_source] Solid contrast, but the Exodus 3 citation needs a URL.
[agent:needs_rewrite] Too dense for recall and stacks several claims that need auditing.
[agent:needs_rewrite] Too dense and leans on unsupplied Christological sourcing.
[agent:needs_source] The counting hook works, but the fifth-objection claim needs a source.
[agent:too_long] Good contrast, but it is longer than it needs to be.
[agent:needs_source] The uraeus history hook needs an auditable source URL.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The image slides into unsourced teaching about God wounding and healing.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The current source does not support the Nile-god claim.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Adds unsourced explanation and overstates with guarantee language.
[agent:unsupported_claim] Adds an unsourced prophet-and-spokesman teaching layer.
[agent:needs_source] The firstborn-as-heir teaching is strong, but the source is missing.
[agent:needs_source] Strong hook, but it needs auditable URLs and is too long.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The image turns into unsourced covenant and judgment commentary.
[agent:unsupported_claim] The Passover foreshadowing and covenant lesson are unsourced here.
